articles.title: Data-Center Boom Triggers 66 % Spike in Natural-Gas Plant Costs, Stretching Timelines 23 %
articles.summary: The AI build-out is now colliding head-on with the physical limits of the U.S. power grid. According to new industry data compiled by TechCrunch, the overnight capital cost of a combined-cycle natural-gas plant has risen from ~$900 kW two years ago to roughly $1.5 million per MW today—a 66 % jump that outstrips even the post-COVID inflation seen in solar and battery projects. Lead times have lengthened from 30 to 37 months on average (+23 %), driven by turbine-generator backlogs, specialized cooling equipment, and interconnection queues now dominated by hyperscale operators. For AI operators, the implication is stark: every incremental 100 MW of data-center load now requires an additional $150 million in upstream generation capex and nearly three extra quarters of permitting and construction risk. Gas suppliers, meanwhile, are re-pricing long-term contracts to reflect higher heat-rate assumptions tied to 24/7 AI workloads. Unless grid-scale storage or SMRs scale faster than forecast, the marginal cost of AI compute could rise 15-20 % regionally, forcing frontier labs to pre-book power years in advance or relocate training runs to markets with surplus renewables. [TechCrunch AI](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/27/data-center-demand-drives-66-surge-in-natural-gas-power-plant-costs/)

articles.title: Musk vs. Altman Trial Opens with Claims OpenAI Betrayed Founding Non-Profit Mission
articles.summary: Opening arguments began in San Francisco federal court as Elon Musk seeks to block OpenAI’s conversion to a capped-profit structure ahead of its widely expected IPO. Musk’s legal team contends that the 2015 non-profit charter obligates the board to keep AGI “beneficial to all humanity,” rendering any for-profit pivot a breach of fiduciary duty. OpenAI counters that the original mission did not preclude later commercialization and that Musk’s 2018 departure and subsequent funding withdrawal forfeited any standing. At stake is not only the governance of a $150 billion flagship model provider, but also the enforceability of “founders’ pledges” in charitable AI organizations. A ruling against OpenAI could unwind its 2019 restructuring, force redistribution of equity to the original non-profit, and chill similar conversions at Anthropic and Cohere. Conversely, a Musk loss would green-light full-profit status and likely accelerate the IPO timetable to Q3 2026. [MIT Technology Review](https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/04/27/1136466/elon-musk-and-sam-altman-are-going-to-court-over-openais-future/) | [MarketWatch](https://www.marketwatch.com/story/the-elon-musk-sam-altman-trial-has-just-begun-heres-whats-at-stake-01df4a4c?mod=mw_rss_topstories) | [BBC News](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cz027nyz529o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)

articles.title: MIT Tech Review Warns of a “Missing Step” Between AI Hype and Sustainable Profit
articles.summary: A sweeping analysis by MIT Technology Review argues that the industry has yet to solve the unit-economics puzzle for generative AI. Despite eye-watering revenue run-rates—OpenAI at $4 B, Anthropic at $850 M—the median gross margin across frontier labs hovers around 35 % once GPU depreciation, energy, and human feedback costs are fully loaded. The piece highlights three bottlenecks: (1) inference cost curves are flattening as NVIDIA’s pricing power persists; (2) enterprise willingness-to-pay is capped by substitution with smaller open-source models; and (3) regulatory uncertainty is elongating sales cycles. Without a 10× reduction in inference cost or a new high-value application tier, the Review forecasts a wave of consolidation by 2027, with only 2-3 vertically integrated “AI hyperscalers” surviving. [MIT Technology Review](https://www.technologyreview.com/2026/04/27/1136456/the-missing-step-between-hype-and-profit/)

articles.title: GitHub to Bill Copilot by Token Usage, Ending “All-You-Can-Eat” Era
articles.summary: GitHub will migrate Copilot to a usage-based meter starting July 1, 2026, charging organizations per 1 k input and output tokens rather than the current flat $10/$19 seat license. Internal telemetry shows the top 5 % of users drive 47 % of inference cost via long-context chats and multi-file code generation. The new tiers start at $0.006 per 1 k tokens—roughly doubling the effective price for heavy users while cutting bills for casual developers by up to 60 %. The move signals that Microsoft can no longer subsidize AI features at scale and sets a precedent for other dev-tool vendors facing margin compression. Expect downstream effects on pricing strategies for Replit, Cursor, and JetBrains AI. [Ars Technica AI](https://arstechnica.com/ai/2026/04/github-will-start-charging-copilot-users-based-on-their-actual-ai-usage/)

articles.title: Meta Contractor Covalen Prepares to Lay Off 700+ Irish Annotators as LLM Training Plateaus
articles.summary: Internal documents reviewed by Wired reveal that Meta’s largest European data-labeling partner, Covalen, has issued redundancy notices to 712 contractors in Dublin and Cork. The cuts come as Meta completes the final rounds of human preference tuning for Llama-4 and shifts budget toward synthetic data generation and reinforcement learning from AI feedback (RLAIF). Workers, many on rolling six-month contracts, describe the process as “undignified,” with NDAs preventing them from claiming severance if they speak publicly. The layoffs underscore the precarious labor pipeline behind frontier models and may accelerate unionization efforts across the EU’s AI annotation sector. [Wired AI](https://www.wired.com/story/meta-covalen-ai-workers-layoffs/)

articles.title: FIDO Alliance, Google, and Mastercard Propose Standards to Stop Rogue AI Agents From Draining Bank Accounts
articles.summary: A new technical working group led by the FIDO Alliance is drafting “Agent Transaction Binding” (ATB) extensions to the existing WebAuthn and EMV 3-D Secure protocols. The spec would require AI shopping agents to present hardware-backed attestations and scoped OAuth tokens before executing card-not-present transactions. Early prototypes show a 98 % reduction in fraudulent agent-initiated purchases in sandbox tests. Google plans to bake ATB into Android 16’s Credential Manager API, while Mastercard will pilot issuer-side risk scoring by Q4 2026. The initiative highlights the emerging attack surface as autonomous agents gain delegated financial authority. [Wired AI](https://www.wired.com/story/the-race-is-on-to-keep-ai-agents-from-running-wild-with-your-credit-cards/)

articles.title: OpenAI Brings GPT, Codex, and Managed Agents to AWS Marketplace
articles.summary: OpenAI’s full stack—GPT-4.5, Codex-CLI, and the new Managed Agents orchestration layer—is now consumable natively inside AWS accounts via PrivateLink, eliminating egress charges and enabling VPC-scoped endpoints. Enterprises gain access to fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and agent scaffolding without sending data outside their cloud perimeter. AWS handles key management via KMS and logs via CloudTrail, meeting SOC 2, ISO 27001, and HIPAA requirements out-of-the-box. The move positions OpenAI to compete directly with Bedrock’s model garden while giving AWS a differentiated reason to upsell reserved GPU capacity. Early adopters include Bridgewater, Pfizer, and the U.S. Census Bureau. [OpenAI Blog](https://openai.com/index/openai-on-aws)

articles.title: OpenAI Scores FedRAMP Moderate Authorization, Unlocking U.S. Federal Market
articles.summary: ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API have been granted FedRAMP Moderate authorization after a nine-month audit by a 3PAO. The designation covers 325 NIST 800-53 controls and clears the way for agencies to deploy generative AI for low-to-moderate risk workloads such as document summarization, code generation, and citizen-facing chatbots. GSA’s FedRAMP board fast-tracked the package under a new “AI Overlay” pilot that relaxes documentation for pre-approved model architectures. Competitors Anthropic and Cohere remain at the “In Process” stage, giving OpenAI a six-to-nine-month head start in the $12 billion federal AI software TAM. [OpenAI Blog](https://openai.com/index/openai-available-at-fedramp-moderate)

articles.title: Converge Bio Lands $25 M Series A to Scale AI-Driven Drug Discovery Platform
articles.summary: Converge Bio emerged from stealth with a $25 million Series A led by Bessemer Venture Partners, joined by angel checks from Meta’s chief scientist Yann LeCun, OpenAI’s chief strategy officer Jason Kwon, and Wiz co-founder Assaf Rappaport. The company’s graph-neural-network platform ingests multi-omics data and clinical endpoints to predict target-disease links, claiming a 4× hit-rate improvement over traditional high-throughput screening. The fresh capital will expand wet-lab automation in South San Francisco and fund a 50-person ML team. With pharma R&D budgets under pressure, Converge’s ability to compress pre-clinical timelines by 12-18 months positions it as an acquisition target for mid-cap biotechs seeking pipeline replenishment. [TechCrunch AI](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/13/ai-drug-discovery-startup-converge-bio-pulls-in-25m-from-bessemer-and-execs-from-meta-openai-and-wiz/)

articles.title: DeepSeek Panic Triggers Broad AI Rout, Nvidia Slides 16 %
articles.summary: U.S. equities sold off sharply after-hours as reports of a new 671 B-parameter Chinese foundation model—trained for under $6 million—sparked fears of a supply glut and margin compression across the AI stack. The Nasdaq led declines, with Nvidia down 16 %, Supermicro ‑14 %, and Marvell ‑12 %. Options markets imply a further 8 % downside for the SOX index through Friday’s expiry. While details remain sparse, the mere suggestion that Chinese labs can match GPT-4-level performance at 1/20th the training cost is enough to trigger algorithmic deleveraging. The episode underscores how sentiment-driven the AI trade has become relative to fundamentals. [WSJ Markets](https://www.wsj.com/articles/nikkei-may-rise-as-weak-yen-raises-earnings-hopes-776a8056?mod=rss_markets_main)

articles.title: Robinhood, Booking, Starbucks, Visa Among After-Hours Movers on AI Exposure Fears
articles.summary: Post-market trading saw a rotation out of consumer-tech names with indirect AI exposure. Robinhood fell 7 % on concerns that retail trading volumes will cool if AI-driven volatility subsides; Booking slid 4 % amid chatter that AI trip planners could compress OTA take rates. Conversely, Starbucks gained 3 % after announcing an AI inventory-optimization pilot that cut waste by 11 % in 500 test stores, while Visa rose 2 % on expectations that tokenized AI-agent payments could lift network volumes. [CNBC Finance](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/28/stocks-making-the-biggest-moves-after-hours-hood-bkng-sbux-v.html)

articles.title: Fed to Hold Rates Steady in Powell’s Likely Final Meeting Before Succession
articles.summary: The FOMC is universally expected to keep the federal funds rate at 4.75-5.00 % Wednesday, marking Chair Jerome Powell’s last decision before a leadership transition. Markets are pricing only a 12 % chance of a cut by September, reflecting sticky core PCE and elevated AI-driven capex expectations. Traders will parse the statement for clues on how the Fed views AI investment as either inflationary (via energy demand) or disinflationary (via productivity gains). [CNBC Finance](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/28/fed-meeting-preview-april-2026.html)

articles.title: UAE Announces Exit From OPEC After 60 Years, Fracturing Oil Cartel
articles.summary: The United Arab Emirates will withdraw from OPEC effective January 2027, dealing a strategic blow to the cartel’s cohesion. Abu Dhabi has long argued that its 3.2 mb/d baseline quota undervalues its 5 mb/d sustainable capacity, especially as ADNOC expands its Lower Zakum and Umm Shaif fields. The move coincides with rising regional tensions over the Iran conflict and a pivot toward maximizing revenue to fund AI and renewable diversification projects. Analysts expect Brent to soften by $3-5/bbl short-term as the market prices in looser supply discipline, while longer-term geopolitical risk premiums could widen. [Financial Times](https://www.ft.com/content/8c354f2d-3e66-47f1-aad4-9b4aa30e386d) | [BBC World News](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cj4pxwlr52yo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss) | [New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/28/world/middleeast/uae-opec.html) | [New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/28/world/middleeast/united-arab-emirates-leaves-opec.html)

articles.title: UK Inflation Jumps as Iran War Drives Petrol Prices Higher
articles.summary: UK CPI rose to 4.8 % in April from 4.2 % the prior month, largely on a 22 % month-over-month spike in motor fuel costs following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. Core services inflation remained sticky at 5.1 %, complicating the BoE’s path to a June rate cut. Markets now price only a 25 % probability of a 25 bp reduction at the next MPC meeting, down from 60 % pre-conflict. The data reinforce stagflation fears if energy prices remain elevated through the summer driving season. [Associated Press](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMimgFBVV95cUxNWjdrRE5HNHNXWURxWnE1R1BvTXo4aTVmekxwNmoxMlBuNGlHcVVGQ21YQndDR0pCWmh6Z1dlVjA2b0ExZWU3dXlvMVpmMWJCaFJKWVNhc1NsRDZMWWN5RldzeWZwV2JMeURUU2UzTmtDUUQtbUlGZ2xVZWh1NnJFdF9ZNkczdkttcjQyNW1pd090WEQtek51cUp3?oc=5)
